summary: Skullmonkeys level-exit teleporters with branching destinations (stage 2a/2b/bonus). Multiple teleporter entities per level, each carrying its own destination -> player chooses by entering one.
branch_chain: 1. Teleporter is a Portal-class entity; player touching it -> PlayerState_LevelExitTeleporter (0x...32620): stops CD stream, marks +0x1b2=1, links entity at player[0x5a].
2. The teleporter entity callback writes the DESTINATION into GameState: +0x19c=1 (branch flag) and +0x19d=<dest level index>.
3. GameStateCollisionCallback (0x...41498): if +0x146 set and +0x19c!=0 -> SetupAndStartLevel(state, *(+0x19d)) = JUMP to that explicit level (NOT sequential advance).
4. Normal level-complete instead sets +0x147 -> SeekToLevelInSequence(.,0,1,0) -> SetupAndStartLevel(99) = next entry in playback sequence.

destination_selection.fixed (Callback_8004906c @ line 15783): uVar2 = teleporter_entity[+0x50] -> GameState+0x19d. Each teleporter is PLACED (Asset 501 variant data) with a hardcoded destination level index. Two/three teleporters in a level = two/three fixed destinations (stage 2a / 2b / bonus). This is the manual branch.
destination_selection.random (Callback_800670bc @ line 31210): rand() % count[+0x67] indexes a destination table param_1[idx+0x60]; rejects repeat of last (+0x19d) by incrementing. A teleporter that sends you to a RANDOM destination from a set (bonus-level roulette).

key_fields.GameState+0x19c: branch flag (1 = use explicit destination instead of sequence advance)
key_fields.teleporter_entity+0x50: this teleporter's fixed destination level index

ties_to: playback sequence + LevelMetadata table (Session 20): destination index -> metadata[idx*0x70] -> that level's assets. Branch just overrides which sequence/metadata entry loads next.
